compartments.name: ce_core
compartments.rationale: All CE primary/backup actions form a closed loop over CE log state. CEViewChange is the view change protocol for CE; CEProcessRecoveredPending integrates OB archiver data back into CE log after recovery. The only external writes are to message channels (msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ce_to_ob, msgs_view_change) and reads from ob_archiver_pending (CEProcessRecoveredPending) and ce_archiver_store (CEViewChange). These are medium-strength interface points captured in I2 and I1.

compartments.name: ob_core
compartments.rationale: All OB per-market actions form a closed loop over OB log and pending log state. OBViewChange implements surgical rollback of OB log (parallel to CEViewChange for C1). OBProducePendingAction and OBReceiveMFR are the CE-downtime and fill-finalization paths respectively. The only external reads are ce_alive (guard in OBProduceEffect), msgs_ce_to_ob (OBReceiveMFR), and ob_archiver_store (OBViewChange recovery). These cross-system reads are captured in I1 (CE channel) and I3 (OB archiver feedback from C3).

compartments.name: oms_downstream_archivers
compartments.rationale: OMS, downstream VC reception, archivers, and message loss are grouped together because: (1) archivers are non-TEE and functionally serve as the recovery substrate for C1/C2 view changes -- splitting them into a 4th compartment would create a fully-connected 4-node topology where every node intersects every other; (2) OMS consumption actions (OMSConsumeCE, OMSConsumeOB, OMSConsumePending) are driven entirely by reading message channels produced by C1 and C2 -- they are downstream consumers with no feedback writes to C1/C2 state except via oms_log (read only by ArchiverIngestOMS which stays in C3); (3) downstream VC reception (DownstreamOB*, DownstreamOMS*) reads msgs_view_change and writes only cache/cursor vars local to C3; (4) message loss actions touch only message channel vars that are already intersection vars. Grouping keeps archivers co-located with OMS whose view change (OMSViewChange) reads both ce_archiver_store and ob_archiver_store.

intersections.directionality: C1 writes msgs_ce_to_ob and msgs_ce_broadcast; C2 writes msgs_ob_to_ce; C1 writes ce_alive which gates OBProduceEffect(Fill) in C2
intersections.notes: Bidirectional channel coupling: CE→OB (MFR delivery via msgs_ce_to_ob), OB→CE (fill notification via msgs_ob_to_ce). ce_alive is a unidirectional gate (C1 writes, C2 reads). msgs_ce_broadcast is shared because OBBackupApply (C2) does not read it -- it only reads msgs_ob_broadcast. Actual C1→C2 channel is msgs_ce_to_ob; msgs_ce_broadcast is a C1→C3 channel but not C1→C2.

intersections.directionality: C1 writes all CE state vars and msgs_ce_broadcast/msgs_view_change; C3 reads them for OMS consumption, archiver ingestion, VC reception, and GC. C3 writes ce_archiver_store (archiver ingest/GC), which C1 reads in CEViewChange for recovery anchor computation.
intersections.notes: This is the strongest intersection. C3 writes back ce_archiver_store which is critical for CEViewChange (C1) recovery. The feedback loop (C1 produces effects → C3 archives them → C1 reads archives on recovery) means proposals that change CE log production or archiver GC logic in either compartment must be reviewed against this intersection. P17 (OMSMutedUntilCaughtUp) and P18 (CEArchiverSafetyGuarantee) are the most complex spanning properties here.

intersections.directionality: C2 writes msgs_ob_broadcast, ob_primary, ob_seq, and OB-tagged msgs_view_change entries; C3 reads them for OMS consumption, archiver ingestion, VC reception, and unmute guard. C3 writes ob_archiver_store and ob_archiver_pending, which C2 reads in OBViewChange (recovery) and C1 reads in CEProcessRecoveredPending.
intersections.notes: ob_primary is read extensively by C3 OMS actions (session guards in OMSConsumeOB, OMSConsumePending, OMSViewChange). ob_seq is the catch-up threshold in OMSUnmute. ob_archiver_pending crosses from C3 into C1 (CEProcessRecoveredPending) -- this means I3 technically has a 3-way touch (C1 also reads ob_archiver_pending), but since ob_archiver_pending is owned/written by C3 and C1 only reads it, this is captured as a note on I2 rather than creating a tri-compartment intersection.

coupling_matrix: C1-C2: weight=6 (msgs_ce_to_ob R/W, msgs_ob_to_ce R/W, ce_alive R, msgs_ce_broadcast partial). C1-C3: weight=16 (msgs_ce_broadcast W/R, ce_primary W/R, ce_last_recovered_seq W/R, ce_authority_ts W, msgs_view_change W/R, ce_log W/R, ce_checkpoint_seq W/R, ce_backup_applied W/R, ce_backup_alive W/R, ce_archiver_store W/R bidirectional). C2-C3: weight=10 (msgs_ob_broadcast W/R, ob_primary W/R, ob_seq W/R, msgs_view_change W/R, ob_archiver_store W/R, ob_archiver_pending W/R).
topology_summary: 3 compartments, 3 intersections, triangle topology, max degree 2 per node. I2 (C1-C3) is the strongest coupling (weight 16) due to archiver store feedback into CE view change recovery. I3 (C2-C3) is strong (weight 10) due to OMS consuming OB state extensively. I1 (C1-C2) is medium (weight 6) with clear bidirectional channel semantics. No weak coupling edges; all 3 intersections carry meaningful shared state. Triangle is acceptable for 3-compartment graph -- fully-connected warning does not apply at this scale.

ambiguities.description: Message loss actions (LoseCEBroadcast etc.) could be treated as a separate 4th compartment or as part of the network/infrastructure layer rather than C3. Placing them in C3 is justified by functional cohesion (they modify channels that C3 actors read) and by keeping compartment count low. However a reviewer might prefer them as a standalone 'network' compartment. The difference is cosmetic: message loss actions touch only message channel vars which are all intersection vars anyway.
ambiguities.resolution: kept in C3; no functional impact on improvement proposals since message loss actions have no improvement surface

ambiguities.description: ob_archiver_pending is written by C3 (ArchiverIngestOBPending) but read by C1 (CEProcessRecoveredPending). This creates a minor C3→C1 dependency not captured in I1. It is noted in I3 (C2-C3) since the pending data originates from OB actions (C2) → archiver (C3) → CE recovery (C1). Treating this as a 3-way intersection point rather than splitting I2 and I3 is the cleaner model.
ambiguities.resolution: captured as note on I3; proposer/reviewer agents should be aware that changes to CEProcessRecoveredPending (C1) or ArchiverIngestOBPending (C3) must check both intersections
